response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with roughly 19 years of hands-on experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, which meets the raw experience bar. However, the role is a director-level individual contributor position focused on AI strategy, and the resume shows no demonstrated AI/ML products or platforms, no recognized AI expertise, no advanced AI-related degree, and no evidence of operating at principal/strategic scope such as advising strategic customers or setting product direction. Recent MCP/agentic-tooling contributions are promising but too early and too thin to support this seniority level.
strengths: ~19 years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency distributed systems
Entrepreneurial track record: founded and maintains an open-source geo-data platform used in production by real organizations
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, open-source libraries, and a history of teaching workshops and giving conference talks
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML products, platforms, or recognized AI expertise beyond recent MCP/agent tooling contributions
Career trajectory is senior/staff IC delivery work, with no evidence of principal-level strategic influence or advising customers/executives
Limited depth in cloud platform strategy; cloud experience is application-level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) rather than platform or product direction

key_factors.factor: AI expertise gap vs. role's core focus
Seniority mismatch (senior IC vs. director-level strategy)
Long, broad engineering experience with entrepreneurial open-source work
